title: Cancer signaling networks in tumor progression and drug resistance: Crosstalk, adaptive reprogramming and therapeutic targeting (Review).
snippet: Cancer signaling networks, highly dynamic with interconnected systems, regulate tumor initiation, its progression, therapeutic response and drug resistance. Rather than functioning as isolated pathways, these networks integrate extracellular and intracellular signals through coordinated interactions among membrane receptors, intracellular transducers and do...

title: Multi-omics profiling of pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors: Interplay of genomic instability and the tumor microenvironment (Review).
snippet: Pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (PNETs) represent a biologically heterogeneous group of neoplasms shaped by both intrinsic genomic alterations and dynamic interactions with the tumor microenvironment (TME). Conventional analytical approaches offered limited insight into these complex mechanisms. However, the emergence of multi-omics technologies including....
